title: Portfolio dashboard shows overall job statistics
description: Verifies the portfolio dashboard loads and displays the four job stat categories (total, pending review, completed, failed).

title: Portfolio dashboard shows next review target details
description: Verifies the next review target section displays a job source and job ID.

steps.description: Verify element "Next review target" is visible

title: Open job workspace from next review target
description: Verifies that using the "Open job workspace" link from the dashboard transitions into a job workspace page with job context visible.

steps.description: Verify element "Open job workspace" is visible

title: Navigate from portfolio to Data Explorer via artifacts link
description: Verifies the "Browse all local job artifacts" link navigates to the Data Explorer page.

steps.description: Verify element "Browse all local job artifacts" is visible
Click on "Browse all local job artifacts"

title: Expand Developer Tools and open Sandbox index
description: Verifies the Developer Tools section expands and the Sandbox index link navigates to the sandbox page.

title: Expand a source node in the job tree to reveal child jobs
description: Verifies that a collapsed source node in the job tree can be expanded and shows child job entries.

steps.description: Verify element "Source" is visible in the job tree
Click on a collapsed source node in the job tree
Verify element "Job" is visible within the expanded source section
Verify at least one child job entry is visible under the expanded source

title: Select a job entry with missing/empty artifacts state
description: Verifies selecting a job without artifacts shows a missing/empty state rather than crashing or rendering blank content.

steps.description: Click on a source node in the job tree to expand it
Click on a job entry that is indicated as having no artifacts

title: Portfolio dashboard remains usable if portfolio summary shows no jobs
description: Verifies that when the dashboard shows an empty portfolio (no jobs), the page displays an empty state and does not block navigation to explorer or developer tools.

steps.description: Verify element "Browse all local job artifacts" is visible
Click on "Browse all local job artifacts"

title: Open Data Explorer and view root data/jobs/ directory listing
description: Verifies the Data Explorer loads and shows the root directory listing with directory metadata visible.

steps.description: Verify element "root directory listing" is visible
Verify element "item count for directories" is visible
Verify element "size indicator for files" is visible

title: Navigate into a folder and verify ?path= updates and folder contents are shown
description: Verifies clicking a folder updates the query parameter and displays the folder contents with item counts.

steps.description: Click on the first visible folder in the listing
Verify element "folder contents list" is visible
Verify element "item count for directories" is visible

title: Preview a JSON file with pretty-printed output
description: Verifies selecting a JSON file shows a readable, pretty-printed JSON preview.

steps.description: Click on the first visible folder in the listing
Click on a visible file with a .json extension
Verify element "file preview panel" is visible

title: Preview a markdown or text file as plain text
description: Verifies selecting a markdown/text file shows a plain-text preview in the preview panel.

steps.description: Click on the first visible folder in the listing
Click on a visible file with a .md or .txt extension
Verify element "file preview panel" is visible
Verify element "size indicator for files" is visible

title: Preview an image file inline (PNG/JPG/GIF/SVG)
description: Verifies selecting an image file renders an inline image preview.

steps.description: Click on the first visible folder in the listing
Click on a visible file with a .png, .jpg, .gif, or .svg extension
Verify element "file preview panel" is visible
Verify element "inline image preview" is visible
Verify element "size indicator for files" is visible

title: Use breadcrumb navigation to go back up the directory tree
description: Verifies the breadcrumb lets the user navigate to a parent directory and updates the listing accordingly.

steps.description: Click on the first visible folder in the listing
Click on the parent level in the breadcrumb navigation
Verify element "root directory listing" is visible

title: Attempt to preview a very large or unsupported file and see a cannot-preview state
description: Verifies the UI gracefully handles files that cannot be previewed by showing a clear message/state and keeping navigation usable.

steps.description: Click on the first visible folder in the listing
Click on a visible file that is indicated as very large or has an unsupported extension
Verify element "file preview panel" is visible
Verify element "size indicator for files" is visible

title: Switch between two different files and verify preview updates each time
description: Verifies the preview panel updates when selecting different files without requiring a page reload.

steps.description: Click on the first visible folder in the listing
Click on a visible file with a .json extension
Click on a visible image file (.png, .jpg, .gif, or .svg)
Verify element "inline image preview" is visible
Verify element "file preview panel" is visible

title: Graph Explorer loads with graph visible in View 1
description: Verifies the job workspace opens and the Graph Explorer (View 1) content renders a visible graph area.

steps.description: Click on the first visible job item in the review queue or job list

title: Selecting a graph edge shows match reasoning below the graph
description: Verifies clicking an edge in the ReactFlow graph reveals match reasoning text in the details area.

steps.description: Click on the first visible job item in the review queue or job list
Click on a visible edge/connection line between two nodes in the graph

title: Match score is visible after selecting an edge
description: Verifies that a match score or score label is surfaced in the UI when an edge is selected.

steps.description: Click on the first visible job item in the review queue or job list
Click on a visible edge/connection line between two nodes in the graph

title: Graph Explorer remains usable after scrolling details area
description: Verifies the details/reasoning region can be scrolled and graph remains visible (no blank/empty state).

steps.description: Click on the first visible job item in the review queue or job list
Click on a visible edge/connection line between two nodes in the graph
Scroll to the bottom of the match reasoning/details section

title: Switching away from View 1 and back preserves the Graph Explorer UI
description: Verifies the user can navigate between workspace views and return to Graph Explorer without losing the graph view.

steps.description: Click on the first visible job item in the review queue or job list

title: View 2 tab loads and shows line-by-line source markdown as selectable lines
description: Verifies the Extraction Review (View 2) renders source markdown content as line-level selectable items.
category: Job Workspace — Document to Graph (Extraction Review, View 2)

steps.description: Click on a job item in the review queue or job tree to open the job workspace
Verify element "a line item button in the source markdown list" is visible

title: Selecting a markdown line shows requirement metadata panel (ID, priority, spans)
description: Verifies clicking a source line reveals the associated requirement details in the side panel.
category: Job Workspace — Document to Graph (Extraction Review, View 2)

steps.description: Click on a job item in the review queue or job tree to open the job workspace
Click on a line item button in the source markdown list

title: Requirement spans are visible after selecting a line
description: Verifies the spans section appears in the requirement panel after a line selection.
category: Job Workspace — Document to Graph (Extraction Review, View 2)

steps.description: Click on a job item in the review queue or job tree to open the job workspace
Click on a line item button in the source markdown list
Verify element "a span entry in the spans list" is visible

title: Selecting a line triggers a visible graph highlight state for the related requirement node
description: Verifies the UI indicates the corresponding requirement node is highlighted after selecting a source line.
category: Job Workspace — Document to Graph (Extraction Review, View 2)

steps.description: Click on a job item in the review queue or job tree to open the job workspace
Click on a line item button in the source markdown list

title: Changing selection updates the requirement panel to a different requirement
description: Verifies selecting another line updates the displayed requirement details (not stale).
category: Job Workspace — Document to Graph (Extraction Review, View 2)

steps.description: Click on a job item in the review queue or job tree to open the job workspace
Click on a line item button in the source markdown list
Click on a different line item button in the source markdown list

title: Inline editing is not available in View 2 and user is guided to Node Editor
description: Verifies that attempting to edit a requirement inline is blocked and the UI indicates to use Node Editor (or no edit controls are present).
category: Job Workspace — Document to Graph (Extraction Review, View 2)

steps.description: Click on a job item in the review queue or job tree to open the job workspace
Click on a line item button in the source markdown list
Verify text "Open Node Editor" is visible
Verify element "an inline edit field for requirement text" is not visible

title: View 2 remains functional after scrolling through long documents
description: Verifies line selection still works after scrolling within the source markdown list.
category: Job Workspace — Document to Graph (Extraction Review, View 2)

steps.description: Click on a job item in the review queue or job tree to open the job workspace
Scroll to the bottom of the source markdown list
Click on a line item button near the bottom of the source markdown list

title: View 2 handles lines with no associated requirement gracefully
description: Verifies selecting a line without a mapped requirement shows a clear empty state or message rather than stale requirement data.
category: Job Workspace — Document to Graph (Extraction Review, View 2)

steps.description: Click on a job item in the review queue or job tree to open the job workspace
Click on a line item that appears non-requirement content (for example, an empty line, heading, or separator)
Verify text "No requirement selected" is visible

title: Smoke: App can load portfolio dashboard as a starting point for manual navigation into a job (non-dynamic route only)
description: Provides a minimal executable smoke check for the app shell to support subsequent manual selection of a job to reach View 3, without using dynamic routes.

title: Outputs tab: open markdown output and verify editor is shown
description: Verifies a user can open the Outputs tab and view a markdown file in an editable textarea.

steps.description: Click on the first visible job folder in the list
Click on the first visible JSON file that looks like a job or pipeline descriptor
Verify the preview panel is visible
Click on any visible link or button labeled "Open" or "Open in workspace"

title: Outputs tab: markdown file loads with contents visible in textarea
description: Verifies that selecting a markdown output (e.g., cv.md) shows its contents in a textarea editor.

steps.description: Click on the first visible job folder in the list
Click on the first visible JSON file that looks like a job or pipeline descriptor
Click on any visible link or button labeled "Open" or "Open in workspace"
Click on the file named "cv.md" in the file list if visible; otherwise click the first file with a ".md" extension

title: Outputs tab: edit markdown and save successfully
description: Verifies that a user can modify markdown content and save the proposed change successfully.

steps.description: Click on the first visible job folder in the list
Click on the first visible JSON file that looks like a job or pipeline descriptor
Click on any visible link or button labeled "Open" or "Open in workspace"
Click on the file named "cv.md" in the file list if visible; otherwise click the first file with a ".md" extension
Type "\n\nTEST_CHANGE: updated by E2E" into the markdown textarea editor

title: Outputs tab: JSON file opens in raw textarea editor
description: Verifies that selecting a JSON output file shows a raw JSON textarea editor.

steps.description: Click on the first visible job folder in the list
Click on the first visible JSON file that looks like a job or pipeline descriptor
Click on any visible link or button labeled "Open" or "Open in workspace"
Click on the first file with a ".json" extension in the file list

title: Outputs tab: attempt to save invalid JSON shows visible error or does not indicate success
description: Verifies that saving invalid JSON does not succeed and shows a visible error or lacks a success indication.

steps.description: Click on the first visible job folder in the list
Click on the first visible JSON file that looks like a job or pipeline descriptor
Click on any visible link or button labeled "Open" or "Open in workspace"
Click on the first file with a ".json" extension in the file list
Type "{ invalid json" into the JSON textarea editor

title: Outputs tab: PNG output preview renders as an image when selected
description: Verifies that selecting a PNG output displays an image preview.

steps.description: Click on the first visible job folder in the list
Click on the first visible JSON file that looks like a job or pipeline descriptor
Click on any visible link or button labeled "Open" or "Open in workspace"
Click on the first file with a ".png" extension in the file list (if present)

title: Outputs tab: switching between files updates the preview/editor accordingly
description: Verifies that selecting different output artifacts updates the right-side preview/editor panel.

steps.description: Click on the first visible job folder in the list
Click on the first visible JSON file that looks like a job or pipeline descriptor
Click on any visible link or button labeled "Open" or "Open in workspace"
Click on the first file with a ".md" extension in the file list
Click on the first file with a ".json" extension in the file list

title: Outputs tab: stage selection changes the file list (if stage selector is present)
description: Verifies that changing the selected stage updates the outputs file list when a stage selector exists.

steps.description: Click on the first visible job folder in the list
Click on the first visible JSON file that looks like a job or pipeline descriptor
Click on any visible link or button labeled "Open" or "Open in workspace"
Select a different option from the stage dropdown (if a stage dropdown is visible)

title: Deployment page shows mission status, checklist section, and submit button present (not assuming completion state)
description: Validates that the Deployment page renders its core sections: mission status, checklist area, and Submit Application button.

steps.description: Click on the first visible job item in the review queue or job tree
Click on "Deployment" in the job workspace navigation

title: Deployment page shows completed-stage indicators (check_circle icons) in checklist
description: Validates that checklist items show completion indicators using check_circle icons when stages are completed.

steps.description: Click on the first visible job item in the review queue or job tree
Click on "Deployment" in the job workspace navigation

title: Submit Application remains disabled when checklist is not fully complete
description: Confirms the Submit Application button is disabled when the deployment checklist is not fully complete.

steps.description: Click on a job item that appears incomplete (for example, a job showing pending/in-progress status in the dashboard indicators)
Click on "Deployment" in the job workspace navigation

title: Deployment page shows resume command section
description: Validates that a resume/continue command is visible to continue the pipeline.

steps.description: Click on the first visible job item in the review queue or job tree
Click on "Deployment" in the job workspace navigation

title: Deployment page shows package file listing section
description: Validates that the package files section renders and shows a list area for packaged outputs.

steps.description: Click on the first visible job item in the review queue or job tree
Click on "Deployment" in the job workspace navigation
Verify element "package files list" is visible

title: All checklist items complete still results in no submission (UI-only placeholder)
description: For a job that appears fully ready, verifies clicking Submit Application does not perform a real submission (known missing endpoint).

steps.description: Click on a job item that appears ready/complete (for example, a job showing ready/complete status in the dashboard indicators)
Click on "Deployment" in the job workspace navigation

title: Deployment page handles missing/empty package files gracefully
description: Validates that if no package files are available, the page still renders and shows an empty-state message or an empty list area.

steps.description: Click on a job item likely to have no packaged outputs (for example, a newly created or early-stage job in the dashboard)
Click on "Deployment" in the job workspace navigation

title: Deployment page displays mission status state text (pending vs ready)
description: Validates that mission status includes visible state text indicating pending/ready status.

steps.description: Click on the first visible job item in the review queue or job tree
Click on "Deployment" in the job workspace navigation

title: Open Sandbox index and see available sandbox tools
description: Verifies the /sandbox page loads and presents a visible list of sandbox tools for developers.

title: Navigate from Sandbox index to Text Tagger sandbox
description: Verifies a user can reach the Text Tagger sandbox from the sandbox index and land on the correct route.

title: Text Tagger sandbox responds to basic text input
description: Verifies the Text Tagger sandbox updates visible state after entering text into the primary input area.

steps.description: Click on the main text input area
Type "Alice worked at Example Corp in 2020." into the main text input area

title: Text Tagger sandbox creates a visible tag from selected text (if supported)
description: Verifies the Text Tagger sandbox can create a visible tag/annotation from text interaction without errors.

steps.description: Click on the main text input area
Type "John Doe is a software engineer." into the main text input area

title: Open Node Editor Sandbox page and verify core editor UI is present
description: Verifies the node editor sandbox loads and shows the primary editor surface and basic controls.

title: Node Editor Sandbox: keyboard shortcuts for undo/redo do not provide global history
description: Verifies that attempting undo/redo via keyboard does not show a full global history behavior (known gap for desired history).

title: Open CV Graph Editor sandbox and verify graph UI is visible
description: Verifies the CV graph editor sandbox loads successfully and displays a visible graph editing surface.

title: CV Graph Editor sandbox: basic interaction does not break page state
description: Verifies basic clicks in the CV graph editor do not navigate away and the editor remains visible.

steps.description: Click on the graph canvas area
